content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Five amendments evaluated against src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs. Amendments bc9d1ee1 and c370455f correctly identify a real resume-path correctness bug where resume_terminal_iterative_stage_result (6348-6360) appends implementer_loop_exited before calling recover_iterative_iteration_result (6231), which hard-fails on a missing sidecar — leaving the run in a permanent StageCommitFailed state with the journal falsely claiming a clean exit. Amendment 72cfeb11 correctly notes that validate_iterative_minimal_loop_setting only rejects zero, so stable_rounds_required > max_consecutive_implementer_rounds silently makes the Stable branch unreachable. Amendment 7f9a313a correctly flags that fs::read on untracked files at 6560 loads full contents into memory twice per iteration. Amendment 53c12bd8 correctly flags that git diff --binary expands binary blobs inline, bloating the hashed diff. All five are real defects with sound remediations.

## Votes

- `fr-8-bc9d1ee1`: Accept
  Verified in engine.rs: resume_terminal_iterative_stage_result (6348-6360) appends implementer_loop_exited before calling recover_iterative_iteration_result (6231), which hard-fails on fs::read_to_string of a missing sidecar. Combined with persist_invocation_parsed_payload_best_effort (6187-6213) swallowing both write failures, a realistic failure window leaves resume unrecoverable while the journal already shows a clean loop exit. The remediation (fall back to re-invoking iteration N, add regression test) is sound — invocation_id is deterministic via the it{iteration} suffix so raw/parsed paths will be recreated.
- `fr-8-c370455f`: Accept
  Same underlying defect as bc9d1ee1 but with a more precise failure-window reconstruction (snapshot write at 6850-6864 becomes durable before stage_completed, producing a terminal-but-sidecarless state on crash). The three proposed alternatives are all viable: fall back to re-invocation (preferred, minimal change), embed parsed payload on the event for redundancy, or escalate sidecar persistence failure to a hard retry. Accepting this alongside bc9d1ee1 gives the implementer flexibility in remediation choice.
- `fr-8-72cfeb11`: Accept
  Confirmed: validate_iterative_minimal_loop_setting (6068-6078) only rejects zero, and validate_iterative_minimal_settings in config.rs (2161-2195) has the same gap. Since stable_count is bounded above by iteration which is bounded by max_rounds, stable_rounds_required > max_rounds makes the Stable branch of iterative_loop_exit_reason (6011) literally unreachable — runs silently degrade to max-rounds termination with no operator signal. Cross-field validation is the correct fix; failing fast at run start in execute_iterative_plan_and_implement_stage is a good belt-and-suspenders.
- `fr-8-7f9a313a`: Accept
  Confirmed at line 6560: fs::read(&absolute_path) loads the entire file into a Vec<u8> before feeding it to the hasher. iterative_untracked_build_artifact (6576) excludes target/ and result* but not arbitrary large untracked files (fixtures, sample data, logs). git_diff_fingerprint runs twice per iteration so peak allocation is unnecessarily high. io::copy with a BufReader into the Sha256 hasher is a drop-in fix with zero semantic change. Real efficiency/robustness issue worth fixing even if current users aren't hitting it.
- `fr-8-53c12bd8`: Accept
  Confirmed: git_diff_fingerprint (6598-6617) passes --binary to both diff commands, which expands binary blobs inline so stdout scales with binary file size. The fingerprint only needs deterministic change detection, not content reproducibility — dropping --binary yields the stable 'Binary files … differ' header that still changes when blobs change. Using git diff --raw tuples is an even cleaner alternative (path + oid pairs). Legitimate memory/CPU concern for repos with lockfiles, generated wasm, images, etc., compounded by the 2x-per-iteration invocation.
